spec_match.rationale: The builder now serializes the `Reporting-Endpoints` header with a dedicated `crash-reporting` endpoint and demonstrates clean routing, legacy mirroring, fallback, and dropped delivery states for the report mix.

interactive.rationale: DevTools exercised all four endpoint URL inputs and both routing checkboxes, with the header, diagnostic text, and five inboxes updating live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index card promises a live header serializer and routing simulation; the page now delivers that plus the before/after motivation for crash-only intake.

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes invalid endpoint omission, missing crash endpoint, default-exclusion drop, CSP named routing, deprecation named routing, and crash mirroring to default.

references.rationale: The page now links to the correct ChromeStatus entry: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5129218731802624.

design_system.rationale: The page uses CSS variables, keeps text/background pairs explicit on badges, and remains an interactive live tool rather than a static card.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on http://localhost:3000/v139/crash-reporting-api-specify-crash-reporting-to-receive-only-crash-reports/header-builder/ in Chrome 148. Pre-fix, DevTools reproduced `Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token ')'`, empty generated header/inboxes, and a ChromeStatus link to the wrong feature id. After the fix, DevTools clicked the CSP named-endpoint checkbox, clicked the exclude-default checkbox, filled default/crash/csp/deprecation endpoint fields, verified crash-only routing, CSP named routing, crash mirroring to default, deprecation endpoint routing, missing crash endpoint drops, invalid default endpoint diagnostics, and restored valid endpoints. Console stayed clean, network requests were expected 200/204 responses, the relevant conformance route loaded with 6/6 pass, and the concept now links to ChromeStatus #5129218731802624.
